title: Present Perfect (since / for)
objectives: Structure short sentences using the present perfect to describe actions and changes that began in the past and continue now
Produce clear present perfect sentences when writing answers and transforming statements

key_concepts.explanation: Form: have/has + past participle. Use for actions that started in the past and continue in the present. Subjects I/You/We/They use 'have'. He/She/It uses 'has'. Negative: haven't/hasn't + past participle. Question: Have/Has + subject + past participle?

Use 'since' to show when the action started (a point in time): since 2020, since last month, since 2010. Use 'for' to show how long the action has continued (a duration): for five years, for twelve years, for many years.
Keywords often used with present perfect: ever, never, just, already, yet, today, since, for.

Affirmative: Saleh has studied English since 2020. Dalal has played the piano for five years. The ice has melted in many polar areas since 2000. Negative: The students haven't finished the science project. Question: Have the animals moved to a cooler place? Where has the water gone?

solution_steps: Subject (Saleh) + has + past participle (studied) + since + point in time (2020)

examples.problem: Dalal has played the piano for five years.
solution_steps: Subject + has + past participle + for + duration
examples.answer: Dalal has played the piano for five years.

exercises.problem: Correct the verbs in brackets and circle since or for: 1. Amal ___ (live) in Oman (since/for) 2014. 2. My teacher ___ (teach) here (since/for) twelve years. 3. I ___ (not see) my best friend (since/for) last month. 4. People ___ (build) cleaner cities (since/for) many years. 5. What have scientists ___ (discover) (since/for) 2010?
exercises.answer: 1. has lived, since. 2. has taught, for. 3. haven't seen, since. 4. have built, for. 5. discovered, since.

exercises.problem: I have visited the new science museum this year. (Form a question)
exercises.answer: Have you visited the new science museum this year?

exercises.problem: He has finished his English homework. (Change into negative)
exercises.answer: He hasn't finished his English homework.

exercises.problem: My World Has Changed: Write 3-4 sentences about any changes that have happened in your life (school, home, weather, etc.). Use the present perfect. Sentence starters: I have started ___ since ___. My ___ has changed because ___. People have ___ for many years. The weather has ___.
exercises.answer: Open-ended personal writing using present perfect

teaching_notes: Grammar lesson on present perfect with since/for. Grammar Box table shows subject-auxiliary-participle-example for affirmative, negative, and question forms. Tip: Use 'have not/has not + past participle' to show an action has not happened up to now. Linker Bank continues. Practice: controlled (correct verb + circle since/for) → transformation (question/negative) → free writing about personal changes. 1 page.
